title: Knowledge & Logic
overview: Logic is how AI knows things — through rules and True/False statements, not common sense

blocks.title: Propositional Logic
blocks.body: Logic is how AI knows things — through rules and True/False statements, not common sense
bullets: Propositional logic uses statements that are either True or False
AND — both sides must be True for result to be True
OR — at least one side must be True
NOT — reverses the value (NOT True = False)

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: A traffic light
leftPanel.description: Red means Stop (True), Green means Go (False for stop)

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
if raining and outside:
    print('Get wet')

blocks.question: Is it true that AND returns True only when both sides are True?
blocks.answer: Yes, AND requires both conditions to be true.

blocks.title: Knowledge Base & Inference
blocks.body: AI stores facts and rules in a Knowledge Base and uses Inference to derive new facts
bullets: A Knowledge Base is a collection of facts and rules AI uses to reason
Inference means deriving new facts from existing ones
IF-THEN rules: If it is raining AND I am outside, THEN I get wet
Real-world uses: medical diagnosis systems, FAQ chatbots, Google Knowledge Panel

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: A doctor's rulebook
leftPanel.description: if symptom A and symptom B then diagnosis C

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent: Knowledge Base + IF-THEN rules → Inference engine → Conclusion

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
if fever and cough:
    print('Possible flu')

blocks.question: Can AI figure out new facts just from existing rules without being told directly?
blocks.answer: Yes, that's exactly what inference is—deriving new facts logically.

blocks.title: How AI Uses Logic
blocks.body: Follow these steps to build a logical reasoning system.
bullets: Step 1: Define your facts — what is True and what is False in your world
Step 2: Write IF-THEN rules — connect facts to conclusions
Step 3: Build a Knowledge Base — store all facts and rules together
Step 4: Run Inference — AI combines facts and rules to reach new conclusions

blocks.question: Do you need to tell AI every possible answer, or can it figure some out from rules?
blocks.answer: It can figure out new answers using inference.

blocks.title: Practice Exercise
blocks.instructions: Write 3 IF-THEN rules about your daily life using AND/OR/NOT
blocks.solution:
# Example:
# if alarm_rings and is_monday:
#     print('Go to class')

keyTakeaways.text: Logic uses True/False statements called propositions
AND needs both sides True; OR needs at least one True; NOT reverses
IF-THEN rules connect facts to conclusions
A Knowledge Base stores all facts and rules AI uses
Inference derives new facts from existing ones — like solving a puzzle
Real uses: medical diagnosis, chatbots, Google Knowledge Panel

whatYouLearned: What propositional logic is and how it works
How AND, OR, NOT operators work in logic
What IF-THEN rules are and how to write them
How AI uses logic in real-world applications
